title: Textures++
description: More mossy blocks... and beyond! Add new textures to any face of any full block.
body:
Textures++ adds **over 20,000** combinations of textures to Minecraft in a survival-friendly, true-to-vanilla way. If you try adding multiple textures to a single block, the number of combinations reaches **over one googol**.

![Large title displaying "Textures++ Datapack" is overlayed above various new textures.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b9503f46df156cc6d53e4b2b55f7d17a0ea97b26.png)

## Description
Textures++ adds **76 new textures** that work like resin clumps—placed on the sides of a block—to spruce up (or, more accurately, moss up) your builds. Using the pack couldn't be easier: just hold a valid item and right click the side of a block to apply a texture. Don't like the texture you placed? Just break the block the texture is sitting on or **scrape** the texture off with your sword.

The following textures can be added to **any full block**:
1. Moss
2. Pale moss
3. Pink petals
4. Wildflowers
5. Lilac vines
6. Rose bush vines
7. Peony vines
8. Weeping vines
9. Twisting vines
10. Pitcher plant vines
11. Cobweb
12. Slime ball
13. Honey block
14. Hanging roots
15. Dirt
16. Gravel
17. Sand
18. Red sand
19. Mud
20. Clay
21. Snow
22. Soul Sand
23. Ancient debris
24. Coal
25. Raw iron
26. Raw copper
27. Raw gold
28. Gold nugget
29. Emerald
30. Lapis lazuli
31. Diamond
32. Redstone dust
33. Quartz
34. Amethyst shard
35. All corals/dead corals (x10)
36. All concrete powders (x16)
37. All wool variants (x16)


## Notes

### Placing Textures
![Player places moss on the side of a white concrete block, making the block appear mossy.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0002aefd37236f73b63dabc4ab4cd5548db69fac.gif)

Right clicking any face of a block should apply its texture to that face of the block. 
- If the texture isn't placed where you expected, make sure you're facing **exactly in the direction** you'd like the texture to be placed. For example, look straight down to place a texture on the top side of the block.
- If the texture is being placed behind the block you expected, make sure the block you're targeting is a **full block**. Textures can't be placed on things like slabs or stairs.
- If the item in your hand is being placed as a block instead of a texture, **get closer to the block**. Some of the items already have place functionality, so you'll need to put yourself in a position where the block can't be placed.

### Removing Textures
![Player scrapes weeping vines off the side of a block, removing the texture from the block.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e2cf4f04a4dd9dc72d6a5a97842ec58ea1525679.gif)

Breaking the block a texture is sitting on will remove all textures from that block. If you want to be more specific, right click any face of a block with a sword to scrape only nearby textures off.

### Offhand
As of version 1.1.0, you are no longer able to place/remove textures while holding a shield or any other tool with right-click functionality in your offhand. This is to prevent the player from being unable to shield while holding a sword.

### Performance
Will adding this datapack to your world impact performance? Not really! Issues begin occuring when roughly 250,000 textures are being rendered at once, or at roughly 60,000 textures if every texture is the same. While this pack offers a **lot** of potential for decoration, it's hard to reach the limit! Having other datapacks installed will lower the texture limit.
